title: Fix For Her page: category circle image sync and body background color

requirements.summary: Fix the sliding product category circles on the For Her page (GirlsHomePage) to fetch and display uploaded category header images from the backend, matching the pattern used in BoysHomePage
acceptanceCriteria: When an image is uploaded for a girls category via the admin panel, the corresponding circle on the For Her page displays that image.
The circle uses the same data-fetching approach as BoysHomePage (fetching category header nav data from the backend).
If no image has been uploaded for a category, the circle falls back to the emoji/icon as before.
Image updates in the admin panel are reflected on the For Her page without requiring a full page reload (React Query cache invalidation).

file_operations.description: Replace the static emoji-based category rendering with a data-driven approach that fetches category headers from the backend using the useCategoryHeaders hook (same pattern as BoysHomePage). For each category in PRODUCT_CATEGORIES filtered to girls, fetch the corresponding CategoryHeader using the category slug. Render the uploaded image using getDirectURL() when available, falling back to the emoji icon when no image has been uploaded. Ensure proper loading states and error handling. The component should use React Query to enable automatic cache invalidation when headers are updated in the admin panel.

requirements.summary: Fix the body background color of the For Her page (GirlsHomePage) to match the background color used on all other customer-facing pages
acceptanceCriteria: The For Her page body background color matches the background color of other customer-facing pages (e.g., HomePage, BoysHomePage).
The header and footer background colors on the For Her page are unchanged.
No off-white or differing background tint is visible in the page body area on the For Her page.

file_operations.description: Update the component's root element background styling to match the standard customer-facing page background. Remove any custom background color classes (such as bg-gray-50, bg-white, or similar off-white tints) from the main container div and ensure it inherits the default body background defined in global styles. Only the page body section should be affected—do not modify any styling applied to the Layout component (header/footer).
